steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that European panda fossils span over 10 million years, and the Bulgarian teeth are described as 'relatively recent' among them. The question asks what the discovery most logically implies about the origin debate.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — pandas presumably originated in some part of Asia before migrating to Europe.: A more recent European fossil cannot establish where pandas first appeared — it only documents later survival. ✗
  • Choice B — the A. nikolovi teeth suggest a later origin for pandas in Europe than was previously believed.: Same problem — recency of evidence cannot determine earlier origins. ✗
  • Choice C — pandas likely originated in Europe since the new species was discovered in Bulgaria.: The teeth are relatively recent, so they do not push the record earlier. ✗
  • Choice D — the A. nikolovi teeth are unlikely to assist in the resolution of the debate surrounding the origins of pandas.: A younger fossil only shows pandas were still present later; it adds nothing to the origin question. ✓
D is correct.
